title: Aozora notation conformance vector
description: One self-contained test case: a source string and the projections a conforming processor must (or should/may) produce. Byte offsets are into the SANITIZED source (the §4 pre-processing output), UTF-8.

name.description: Stable case identifier; matches the containing directory name.

level.description: Conformance level (§10). `must`: a conforming processor MUST match every listed projection. `should`/`may` scale down.

spec_section.description: Section that defines this behaviour, e.g. "6.1".

note.description: REQUIRED provenance tag. MUST begin with `[provenance:…]` naming the hand-derivation source (spec section + AOZORA-ANNOTATION snapshot, optionally a corpus citation). This is the de-circularisation contract: a vector's expected output is hand-derived from the spec and the corpus — never imported from a processor's own output.

work.description: Corpus-relative path the `source` fragment was taken from, e.g. "作品/著者/作品.txt".

line.description: 1-based line in `work` where the fragment occurs (best-effort pointer).

corpus.description: Optional citation for a vector whose `source` is a real corpus fragment. When present and $AOZORA_CORPUS_ROOT is set, the validator confirms `source` actually occurs in `work` (corpus-verify) — turning provenance from a claim into a checked fact.

source.description: Raw input. May contain pre-sanitize bytes (BOM, CRLF) when the case exercises §4.

expected.description: Projections. A `must` vector MUST carry `html`, `serialize`, and `diagnostics`; `nodes`/`pairs`/`container_pairs` are present when the case asserts structure.

html.description: Reference HTML rendering (§8). Informative unless the case's level pins it.

nodes.description: Recognised nodes in source order, each with its SOURCE-coordinate span. Container open/close appear as `containerOpen`/`containerClose` nodes.

pairs.description: Delimiter pairs (ruby, quotes) with source spans for each side.
